_meta.cycle_disposition_reason: Weekly research bundle contains multiple regime-level structural signals: USTR Section 301 legal re-architecture (76 investigations), Fed easing bias rejection amid stagflation, ECB hold with intensified dual risks, and 52% European gas price surge. These developments span four indicator domains (trade_tariff, inflation_central_bank, financial_stability, growth_recession) and represent material change under methodology rules despite stable reasoner postures.

lead_signal.headline: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations targeting forced labor and excess capacity, re-architecting US tariff regime post-IEEPA nullification
lead_signal.body: Following the February 2026 Supreme Court ruling that struck down IEEPA-based tariffs, the US Trade Representative has launched 76 new Section 301 trade investigations covering forced labor enforcement failures and structural excess manufacturing capacity across dozens of economies. This represents a fundamental legal re-architecture of the US tariff regime: Section 301 is significantly harder to challenge in court than IEEPA, making this the most durable tariff escalation vector yet deployed. The administration is simultaneously relying on Section 122 balance-of-payments authority for an interim 10% blanket tariff that expires in approximately 150 days, creating a policy cliff risk in Q3 2026. Market pricing appears to treat the BOP expiry as a potential de-escalation catalyst; GMM assesses the Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely.

module_0.title: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations, re-architecting US tariff regime with legally durable foundation
module_0.body: The US Trade Representative has launched 76 new Section 301 trade investigations following the Supreme Court's February 2026 nullification of IEEPA-based tariffs. Sixty investigations target inadequate foreign enforcement against forced labor; 16 target structural excess manufacturing capacity. This is not a marginal escalation event but a regime-level legal restructuring: Section 301 is harder to challenge in court than IEEPA, per Peterson Institute analysis. The administration is using Section 122 balance-of-payments authority for an interim 10% blanket tariff expiring in roughly 150 days. Market pricing treats the BOP expiry as a de-escalation catalyst; GMM assesses the Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely. This affects six asset classes: Bonds, Tech, Metals, EM Equities, Consumer Staples, and Real Estate.

mainstream.headline: Federal Reserve holds rates at 3.50-3.75% at April 29 FOMC, explicitly rejects easing bias
mainstream.why_it_matters: The FOMC voted unanimously to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.50-3.75% and declined to include an easing bias in the statement, signaling that the dual-mandate tension between tariff-driven inflation and slowing growth remains unresolved. January 2026 FOMC minutes had flagged upside rate risk if inflation remained above target. This is a regime-level signal: the Fed is not prepared to provide growth insurance against tariff damage, which structurally tightens financial conditions relative to pre-tariff baselines. Fed funds futures have been pricing 2-3 cuts in H2 2026; GMM assesses this pricing is premature given the easing bias rejection and tariff-driven inflation running above target.
mainstream.body: The FOMC voted unanimously to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.50-3.75% and declined to include an easing bias in the statement, signaling that the dual-mandate tension between tariff-driven inflation and slowing growth remains unresolved. January 2026 FOMC minutes had flagged upside rate risk if inflation remained above target. This is a regime-level signal: the Fed is not prepared to provide growth insurance against tariff damage, which structurally tightens financial conditions relative to pre-tariff baselines. Fed funds futures have been pricing 2-3 cuts in H2 2026; GMM assesses this pricing is premature given the easing bias rejection and tariff-driven inflation running above target.
mainstream.asymmetric: Market pricing extended hold or possible hike tail as low probability; GMM assesses dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary tariff shock makes cuts structurally unlikely in 2026.

mainstream.headline: ECB holds all three key rates unchanged at April 30 meeting, cites Middle East war dual impact on inflation and growth
mainstream.why_it_matters: The ECB Governing Council held all three key rates unchanged, citing the Middle East war's dual impact: upside inflation risk from energy prices and downside growth risk from confidence and supply chain disruption. ECB President Lagarde explicitly rejected the stagflation framing, noting euro area GDP grew 0.1% in Q1 2026 and projecting 0.9% growth for the full year. However, the ECB's own scenario analysis acknowledges that prolonged energy supply disruption would push inflation above and growth below baseline. Market OIS curve had priced roughly one-third probability of a 25bp cut by end-2026; GMM assesses this cut probability is overstated given intensified upside inflation risks.
mainstream.body: The ECB Governing Council held all three key rates unchanged, citing the Middle East war's dual impact: upside inflation risk from energy prices and downside growth risk from confidence and supply chain disruption. ECB President Lagarde explicitly rejected the stagflation framing, noting euro area GDP grew 0.1% in Q1 2026 and projecting 0.9% growth for the full year. However, the ECB's own scenario analysis acknowledges that prolonged energy supply disruption would push inflation above and growth below baseline. Market OIS curve had priced roughly one-third probability of a 25bp cut by end-2026; GMM assesses this cut probability is overstated given intensified upside inflation risks.
mainstream.asymmetric: Market pricing residual easing; ECB guidance suggests extended hold with hike scenarios in play under adverse energy scenarios.

mainstream.headline: European gas prices surge 52% between February and March ECB meetings, embedding energy shock in inflation projections
mainstream.why_it_matters: ECB accounts from the March 18-19 Governing Council meeting confirm European gas prices rose 52% between the February and March policy meetings, compounded by low inventory levels. The ECB's March projections revised headline inflation up to 2.6% for 2026 from 1.9% in December 2025 projections, with the energy channel identified as the primary driver. The ECB's scenario analysis explicitly warns that prolonged oil and gas supply disruption would push inflation above and growth below baseline. This is a structural supply-side shock with multi-quarter transmission, altering the inflation baseline for the eurozone regime.
mainstream.body: ECB accounts from the March 18-19 Governing Council meeting confirm European gas prices rose 52% between the February and March policy meetings, compounded by low inventory levels. The ECB's March projections revised headline inflation up to 2.6% for 2026 from 1.9% in December 2025 projections, with the energy channel identified as the primary driver. The ECB's scenario analysis explicitly warns that prolonged oil and gas supply disruption would push inflation above and growth below baseline. This is a structural supply-side shock with multi-quarter transmission, altering the inflation baseline for the eurozone regime.
mainstream.asymmetric: Energy shock is embedding structurally in eurozone inflation baseline, not a transitory spike; this constrains ECB easing capacity for at least 12 months.
mainstream.source_label: European Central Bank Governing Council Accounts

underweighted.headline: Section 301 legal durability creates multi-year tariff baseline shift, not cyclical trade policy adjustment
underweighted.why_it_matters: The shift from IEEPA to Section 301 as the primary tariff authority is being treated by markets as a procedural adjustment. GMM assesses this is a fundamental legal re-anchoring: Section 301 is harder to challenge in court, and the 76 investigations cover structural issues (forced labor, excess capacity) that cannot be resolved quickly. The BOP authority tariffs expire in approximately 150 days, creating a policy cliff that markets are pricing as a de-escalation catalyst. GMM assesses the Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely; the BOP cliff is a transition mechanism, not a relief valve. This has material implications for duration positioning in EM sovereign credit and for supply chain cost baselines in Tech and Consumer Staples.
underweighted.body: The shift from IEEPA to Section 301 as the primary tariff authority is being treated by markets as a procedural adjustment. GMM assesses this is a fundamental legal re-anchoring: Section 301 is harder to challenge in court, and the 76 investigations cover structural issues (forced labor, excess capacity) that cannot be resolved quickly. The BOP authority tariffs expire in approximately 150 days, creating a policy cliff that markets are pricing as a de-escalation catalyst. GMM assesses the Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely; the BOP cliff is a transition mechanism, not a relief valve. This has material implications for duration positioning in EM sovereign credit and for supply chain cost baselines in Tech and Consumer Staples.
underweighted.asymmetric: Market mispricing BOP expiry as relief; Section 301 durability creates multi-year elevated tariff baseline affecting global trade costs structurally.

underweighted.headline: Fed easing bias rejection signals dual-mandate paralysis, not data-dependent flexibility
underweighted.why_it_matters: The April 29 FOMC decision to explicitly reject an easing bias is being interpreted by markets as temporary caution pending more data. GMM assesses this signals a deeper structural problem: the Fed is caught between tariff-driven inflation running above target and tariff-driven growth slowing below potential. The January 2026 FOMC minutes flagged upside rate risk if inflation remained elevated; the April statement confirms this tail risk remains active. Fed funds futures pricing 2-3 cuts in H2 2026 appears inconsistent with a central bank that has explicitly removed easing bias and is facing a stagflationary supply shock. This is underweighted because consensus still treats the Fed as data-dependent and flexible; GMM assesses the Fed is structurally constrained by the dual-mandate bind.

GMM assesses this signals a deeper structural problem: the Fed is caught between tariff-driven inflation running above target and tariff-driven growth slowing below potential. This is underweighted because consensus still treats the Fed as data-dependent and flexible; GMM assesses the Fed is structurally constrained by the dual-mandate bind.
underweighted.asymmetric: Dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary tariff shock makes cuts structurally unlikely; market pricing extended hold or hike tail as low probability is a material divergence.

underweighted.headline: EU-US Turnberry tariff deal locks in 15% rate, shifting trade drag from uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss
underweighted.why_it_matters: The Turnberry deal fixes US tariffs on EU exports at 15%, reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness as tariffs on rivals like China converge to similar levels. EU export growth to the US is projected to slow 4.6% in 2026 due to higher effective rates and relative disadvantage. This shifts the trade drag from episodic uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss. Markets are treating the deal as a de-escalation signal; GMM assesses it solidifies the tariff shock as a cyclical headwind rather than resolving it. This is underweighted because consensus focuses on the uncertainty reduction, not the competitiveness erosion.

underweighted.asymmetric: Turnberry deal solidifies tariff shock as competitiveness drag, not episodic uncertainty; EU export growth slowdown is structural, not transitory.

US fiscal dominance tail risk is under-covered by consensus: 9.8 trillion USD Treasury maturity wall plus 1 trillion USD interest meets tariff-fueled inflation, potentially forcing Fed to accommodate fiscal needs over price stability mandate.

BIS USD debt loop dynamics are amplifying sovereign stress in EM commodity importers as dollar strength and reserve depletion constrain refinancing capacity. No new IMF Article IV or BIS guidance published this week, but prior FSB recommendations on NBFI stress remain active as private credit gate events and real M2 negative question AI capex resilience.

threshold_assessments.title: PIIE analysis confirms USTR Section 301 legal re-architecture post-IEEPA nullification
threshold_assessments.summary: PIIE analysis published May 7, 2026 confirms USTR launched 76 Section 301 investigations as the administration seeks a legally durable tariff foundation after the February 2026 Supreme Court ruling struck down IEEPA-based tariffs. Section 301 is assessed as significantly harder to challenge in court than IEEPA. The BOP authority tariffs currently in place expire after 150 days unless extended by Congress, creating a policy cliff risk in Q3 2026.
threshold_assessments.asymmetric: Market pricing treats BOP expiry as de-escalation catalyst; PIIE analysis suggests Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely.

EU export growth to the US is projected to slow 4.6% in 2026 due to higher effective rates and relative disadvantage.
threshold_assessments.asymmetric: Turnberry deal solidifies tariff shock as competitiveness drag rather than episodic uncertainty; markets treating as de-escalation signal.

vectors.name: Policy Rate Divergence
vectors.headline: Fed and ECB both hold rates but reject easing bias, signaling dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary shock
vectors.summary: Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% and explicitly rejects easing bias; ECB holds all three key rates unchanged and cites intensified upside inflation risks. Both central banks are caught between tariff-driven inflation and slowing growth.
vectors.detail: The Federal Reserve and European Central Bank both held rates unchanged this week but explicitly rejected easing bias, signaling dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary tariff and energy shocks. Fed funds futures pricing 2-3 cuts in H2 2026 appears inconsistent with the Fed's easing bias rejection and tariff-driven inflation running above target. ECB market OIS pricing one-third probability of 25bp cut by end-2026 is overstated given intensified upside inflation risks from 52% gas price surge.
vectors.asymmetric: Market pricing extended hold or hike tail as low probability; GMM assesses dual-mandate paralysis makes cuts structurally unlikely in 2026.

vectors.detail: Emerging market sovereign spreads are widening under the triple headwind of IMF growth downgrade to 3.9%, BIS USD debt loop dynamics, and Section 301 investigations targeting EM exporters. Section 301 durability creates multi-year elevated tariff baseline affecting EM debt sustainability. US fiscal dominance tail risk is under-covered: 9.8 trillion USD Treasury maturity wall plus 1 trillion USD interest meets tariff-fueled inflation, potentially forcing Fed to accommodate fiscal needs over price stability mandate.
vectors.asymmetric: Fiscal dominance tail risk under-covered; Section 301 durability creates multi-year elevated tariff baseline affecting EM debt sustainability.

vectors.name: Currency Regime Stress

vectors.summary: EM FX depreciation is accelerating as Section 301 investigations target forced labor and excess capacity in EM exporters, dollar safe-haven bid remains intact, and reserve depletion signals sovereign stress.
vectors.detail: Currency regime stress is highest in EM economies targeted by Section 301 investigations and facing BIS USD debt loop dynamics. EM FX depreciation is accelerating as reserve depletion signals sovereign stress. Dollar safe-haven bid remains intact despite partial FX swap basis normalization, which reasoner assesses as episodic TS noise rather than structural SA movement. Extended dollar strength tightens global financial conditions and constrains EM FX, amplifying sovereign stress in commodity importers.
vectors.asymmetric: BIS USD debt loop and IMF 3.9% EM growth downgrade corroborate structural headwind for EM FX; dollar weaponization continues.

vectors.name: Private Credit Cascade

vectors.summary: Private credit gate events are emerging as real M2 turns negative post-CPI 3.3% and tariff supply chain stress hits Tech hardware chains. AI capex resilience is questioned.
vectors.detail: Private credit gate events are emerging as real M2 turns negative post-CPI 3.3% and Section 301 investigations directly target supply chains affecting Tech hardware. Blind Spot 2 (Nominal M2 Mirage) is active, suppressing score realism for Crypto and Tech. AI capex resilience is questioned as tariff supply chain costs escalate and liquidity deteriorates. Reasoner flags this as LOW urgency cross-monitor signal to AGM, but GMM assesses it as ELEVATED risk vector given the confluence of private credit stress, negative real M2, and tariff supply chain disruption.
vectors.asymmetric: AI capex resilience questioned as Section 301 escalates supply chain costs; Blind Spot 2 active suppresses score realism.

module_9.title: Trade and Tariff Policy
module_9.digest_note: This week marks a regime-level shift in US tariff policy architecture: the USTR has launched 76 Section 301 investigations following the Supreme Court's February 2026 nullification of IEEPA-based tariffs. Section 301 is harder to challenge legally than IEEPA, making this the most durable tariff escalation vector yet deployed.

new_developments.title: USTR launches 76 Section 301 investigations targeting forced labor and excess capacity across dozens of economies
new_developments.summary: Following the February 2026 Supreme Court ruling that struck down IEEPA-based tariffs, the USTR launched 76 new Section 301 investigations covering forced labor enforcement failures and structural excess manufacturing capacity. Sixty investigations target inadequate foreign enforcement against forced labor; 16 target structural excess manufacturing capacity. Section 301 is assessed by PIIE as significantly harder to challenge legally than IEEPA, making this the most durable tariff escalation vector yet deployed.
new_developments.enforcement: Investigations launched; tariffs to follow upon completion of Section 301 process
new_developments.asymmetric: Market pricing BOP expiry as de-escalation catalyst; GMM assesses Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely. This affects six asset classes: Bonds, Tech, Metals, EM Equities, Consumer Staples, and Real Estate.

new_developments.title: EU-US Turnberry deal locks in 15% tariff rate on EU exports, shifting trade drag from uncertainty to competitiveness loss
new_developments.summary: The Turnberry deal fixes US tariffs on EU exports at 15%, reducing uncertainty but eroding EU competitiveness as tariffs on rivals like China converge to similar levels. EU export growth to the US is projected to slow 4.6% in 2026 due to higher effective rates and relative disadvantage. This shifts the trade drag from episodic uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss.
new_developments.enforcement: In force; European Parliament endorsed deal on April 19, 2026, pending national ratification
new_developments.asymmetric: Turnberry deal solidifies tariff shock as competitiveness drag rather than episodic uncertainty; markets treating as de-escalation signal, but GMM assesses it as structural headwind.

new_developments.title: USTR 2026 Trade Policy Agenda doubles down on America First tariffs, signals sustained high-tariff regime
new_developments.summary: USTR 2026 Trade Policy Agenda emphasizes enforcing existing tariffs, monitoring ART commitments, and USMCA review to address worker impacts. Highlights trade deficit shift with EU surpassing China. Signals sustained high-tariff regime without de-escalation.
new_developments.enforcement: Policy framework in force; USMCA Joint Review in progress
new_developments.asymmetric: Confirms tariff shock as entrenched structural policy; USMCA review pressures EU coordination and enforcement hits EM overcapacity.

new_developments.title: EU steel and aluminum face sustained 25% and 10% US Section 232 tariffs with no resolution in sight
new_developments.summary: Ongoing Section 232 tariffs at 25% on steel and 10% on aluminum create high costs for EU auto, machinery, and construction exporters. Part of overlapping regimes with no resolution in sight despite negotiations. Drives input inflation for US manufacturing.
new_developments.asymmetric: Perpetuates metals input cost pressure amid broader tariff cascade; Section 232 negotiation progress should be monitored.

module_9.friction_analysis: Trade friction is highest in the US-China-EU triangle this week. The USTR's 76 Section 301 investigations target forced labor and excess capacity across dozens of economies, with China and EM exporters most acutely affected. The EU-US Turnberry deal locks in 15% tariffs, shifting the trade drag from uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss. EU steel and aluminum face sustained 25% and 10% Section 232 tariffs with no resolution in sight. The USTR 2026 Trade Policy Agenda confirms the tariff shock as entrenched structural policy, with USMCA review adding pressure on EU coordination.
module_9.tariff_escalation_rung: Tariff escalation rung remains at T3 (Enacted tariffs with active retaliation). The USTR's 76 Section 301 investigations represent a legal re-architecture of the tariff regime rather than a rung escalation per se, but the durability of Section 301 as a legal foundation means the effective tariff baseline is rising structurally. The BOP authority tariffs expire in approximately 150 days, creating a policy cliff risk in Q3 2026. Market pricing treats the BOP expiry as a de-escalation catalyst (potential move to T2); GMM assesses the Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely, with T4 (Broad-based tariff war) as the more probable trajectory if Section 301 investigations yield tariffs as expected.

retaliation_chain.measure: Retaliatory duties on US agricultural products
retaliation_chain.macro_impact: Adds to US agricultural sector stress; limited macro transmission given sector size
Contributes to US export slowdown; Section 301 investigations targeting China excess capacity likely to trigger further retaliation

multilateral_signals.item: IMF downgrades EM growth forecast to 3.9% for 2026
multilateral_signals.summary: IMF downgraded EM growth forecast to 3.9% for 2026, corroborating structural headwind from Section 301 tariff durability and BIS USD debt loop dynamics. This is a material downgrade from prior baseline and signals intensified sovereign stress in EM commodity importers.
multilateral_signals.asymmetric: IMF downgrade corroborates GMM assessment that Section 301 durability creates multi-year elevated tariff baseline affecting EM debt sustainability.

central_bank_coordination.item: Fed and ECB both reject easing bias, signaling dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary shock
central_bank_coordination.summary: Fed holds at 3.50-3.75% and explicitly rejects easing bias at April 29 FOMC; ECB holds all three key rates unchanged at April 30 meeting and cites intensified upside inflation risks. Both central banks are caught between tariff-driven inflation and slowing growth, signaling dual-mandate paralysis. This is a divergence from market expectations of coordinated easing in H2 2026.

fiscal_governance.item: US Treasury maturity wall of 9.8 trillion USD plus 1 trillion USD interest creates fiscal dominance tail risk
fiscal_governance.summary: US Treasury maturity wall of 9.8 trillion USD plus 1 trillion USD interest creates fiscal dominance tail risk. CBO FY2026 numbers plus tariff pass-through break Fed independence tail risk. Bank reserves at 3.2 trillion USD provide cushion but are thinning. This is under-covered by consensus as focus remains on Iran war.

governance_gaps.gap: No multilateral framework for Section 301 tariff dispute resolution
governance_gaps.analysis: The USTR's shift from IEEPA to Section 301 as the primary tariff authority creates a governance gap: Section 301 is a unilateral US trade remedy with no multilateral dispute resolution framework. The WTO dispute settlement mechanism is effectively non-functional due to Appellate Body paralysis. This means affected economies have no effective legal recourse against Section 301 tariffs, creating a structural power asymmetry in global trade governance.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: Section 301 durability is amplified by the absence of effective multilateral dispute resolution; this governance gap makes tariff escalation structurally more likely.

governance_gaps.gap: No coordinated central bank response to stagflationary tariff and energy shocks
governance_gaps.analysis: Fed and ECB both reject easing bias but offer no coordinated framework for managing stagflationary shocks. The absence of coordinated central bank response to tariff-driven inflation and energy-driven supply shocks creates a governance gap: each central bank is optimizing domestically, but the global spillovers (dollar strength, EM FX stress, sovereign debt sustainability) are unaddressed.
governance_gaps.asymmetric: Lack of central bank coordination amplifies EM FX stress and sovereign debt sustainability concerns; BIS USD debt loop dynamics are unaddressed.

module_11.title: Scenario and Tail Risk

items.title: Section 301 tariff pipeline creates durable greater than 15% effective rate post-BOP expiry; market mispricing as de-escalation
items.probability_delta: Probability increased this week as USTR launched 76 Section 301 investigations; market continues to misprice BOP expiry as de-escalation catalyst
items.asymmetric: Section 301 durability creates multi-year elevated tariff baseline; BOP cliff is transition mechanism, not relief valve. Affects Metals, Tech, EM Equities most acutely. Reasoner assesses likelihood 0.75, impact 0.85.

items.title: US Treasury maturity wall of 9.8 trillion USD plus 1 trillion USD interest meets tariff-fueled inflation; forces fiscal dominance
items.probability_delta: Probability stable this week but remains under-covered by consensus; focus on Iran war rather than structural supply imbalance
items.asymmetric: CBO FY2026 numbers plus tariff pass-through break Fed independence. Bank reserves 3.2 trillion USD cushion thinning. Reasoner assesses likelihood 0.65, impact 0.90. Non-consensus tail risk.

items.title: Prolonged Middle East energy supply disruption pushes eurozone inflation above and growth below baseline
items.probability_delta: Probability stable this week; ECB scenario analysis explicitly warns of this tail risk
items.asymmetric: 52% gas price surge already embedding in inflation projections; prolonged disruption would force ECB into extended hold or hike despite growth slowdown. Stagflation scenario for eurozone.

structural_trends.title: Legal re-architecture of US tariff regime from IEEPA to Section 301
structural_trends.trend: The shift from IEEPA to Section 301 as the primary tariff authority is a structural trend with multi-year implications. Section 301 is harder to challenge in court and covers structural issues (forced labor, excess capacity) that cannot be resolved quickly. This creates a durable elevated tariff baseline for global trade costs.

structural_trends.title: Dual-mandate paralysis at major central banks under stagflationary shock
structural_trends.trend: Fed and ECB both rejecting easing bias signals a structural trend: major central banks are caught between tariff-driven inflation and slowing growth, with no clear policy path. This dual-mandate paralysis tightens financial conditions structurally and constrains policy flexibility for at least 12 months.

structural_trends.title: Energy shock embedding structurally in eurozone inflation baseline
structural_trends.trend: 52% European gas price surge between February and March is embedding structurally in eurozone inflation projections, with ECB revising 2026 headline inflation up to 2.6% from 1.9%. This is a multi-quarter transmission with structural implications for ECB policy and eurozone growth trajectory.

macro_health_composite.note: Macro health composite deteriorating as Section 301 tariff re-architecture, dual-mandate paralysis at Fed and ECB, and 52% European gas price surge create stagflationary headwinds across growth, inflation, and financial stability domains

key_judgments.judgment: The USTR's launch of 76 Section 301 investigations represents a regime-level legal re-architecture of the US tariff regime, not a marginal escalation event. Section 301 is harder to challenge in court than IEEPA, making this the most durable tariff escalation vector yet deployed. Market pricing treats the BOP expiry as a de-escalation catalyst; GMM assesses the Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely.

The Federal Reserve's explicit rejection of an easing bias at the April 29 FOMC signals dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary tariff shock. Fed funds futures pricing 2-3 cuts in H2 2026 appears inconsistent with the Fed's easing bias rejection and tariff-driven inflation running above target. This is a material divergence between market pricing and central bank guidance.

The 52% European gas price surge between February and March ECB meetings is embedding structurally in eurozone inflation projections, with the ECB revising 2026 headline inflation up to 2.6% from 1.9%. This is a multi-quarter transmission with structural implications for ECB policy and eurozone growth trajectory. Market OIS pricing one-third probability of 25bp cut by end-2026 is overstated.

The EU-US Turnberry deal locks in 15% tariffs, shifting the trade drag from episodic uncertainty to structural competitiveness loss. EU export growth to the US is projected to slow 4.6% in 2026. Markets are treating the deal as a de-escalation signal; GMM assesses it solidifies the tariff shock as a cyclical headwind rather than resolving it.

IMF downgrade of EM growth forecast to 3.9% for 2026 corroborates structural headwind from Section 301 tariff durability and BIS USD debt loop dynamics. EM sovereign spreads are widening under the triple headwind of IMF growth downgrade, BIS USD debt loop, and Section 301 investigations targeting EM exporters. Section 301 durability creates multi-year elevated tariff baseline affecting EM debt sustainability.

US Treasury maturity wall of 9.8 trillion USD plus 1 trillion USD interest creates fiscal dominance tail risk. CBO FY2026 numbers plus tariff pass-through break Fed independence tail risk. This is under-covered by consensus as focus remains on Iran war. Reasoner assesses likelihood 0.65, impact 0.90.

blind_spots.name: Earnings Suppression
blind_spots.description: Q1 pre-tariff earnings beats mask Q2 guidance risk as Section 301 investigations directly target supply chains affecting Tech hardware. EPS revision breadth -15% confirms distortion. Markets treating earnings resilience as structural when it is cyclical lag.

blind_spots.name: Nominal M2 Mirage
blind_spots.description: H.6 M2 approximately 1.8-2.0% YoY but real M2 negative post-CPI 3.3%. Forward risk intact for Crypto and Tech as liquidity deterioration masked by nominal M2. Markets treating nominal M2 as supportive when real M2 is contractionary.

BOP authority tariffs expire in approximately 150 days. Market pricing BOP expiry as de-escalation catalyst; GMM assesses Section 301 pipeline makes de-escalation structurally unlikely.

Dual-mandate paralysis under stagflationary tariff shock makes cuts structurally unlikely.
